title: Enigma Stage 2 Postmortem — Evolutionary Optimizer Search
objective: Test code-level and DSL mutations on top of Stage 1 merged winners, validated on real NanoChat GPT-2 training with ClimbMix-400B dataset

hypotheses_killed.H15: Weak mechanism — per-matrix norm already handled by Polar Express

hypotheses_demoted.H12: torch.compile too complex for Stage 2, reserve for Stage 3
hypotheses_demoted.H17: Adaptive per-group clip partially covered by stateful control

portfolio.title: bfloat16 Polar Express dtype (match production)
portfolio.mechanism: Production Muon casts to bfloat16 for Polar Express; candidate uses float32

portfolio.title: Widen trust ratio clamp [0.1, 8.0]
portfolio.mechanism: Stage 1 trust won with tight [0.5, 1.5]. Test if wider range amplifies

portfolio.title: Amplify gate step_fraction annealing
portfolio.mechanism: Push step_fraction from -1.0 to -2.0, bias +0.5, sharpness 1.5

portfolio.title: Reorder: trust_ratio before second_moment
portfolio.mechanism: Trust ratio should see raw orthogonalized norms, not second_moment-rescaled ones

portfolio.title: Cautious WD gated by trust threshold
portfolio.mechanism: Only apply cautious WD where trust >= 0.8

portfolio.title: Disable second_moment, rely on trust+clip
portfolio.mechanism: Tests whether NorMuon variance reduction is essential when trust+clip are present

phase1_toy_screening.purpose: Crash detection and basic sanity check
phase1_toy_screening.reliability: LOW — toy backend uses fake model with random data. H18 showed massive win (+0.27) that completely reversed on real training.

phase2_real_shallow.purpose: Real NanoChat validation at reduced depth
phase2_real_shallow.reliability: MEDIUM — real training but shallow depth (4 vs 12 layers). H18 won here but lost at full depth.

phase3_gpt2_200steps.purpose: Full GPT-2 scale head-to-head of ALL 14 mutations (Stage 1 + Stage 2)
phase3_gpt2_200steps.reliability: HIGH — real NanoChat, full GPT-2 depth, 200 steps, all seeds identical

phase4_gpt2_1000steps.reliability: HIGHEST — real NanoChat, full GPT-2 depth, 1000 steps, definitive

overall_winner.title: Trust ratio (layerwise, clamp [0.5, 1.5])
overall_winner.consistency: Won at 20 steps (Stage 1), 200 steps (rank #2), and 1000 steps (rank #1)

ranking_evolution_across_horizons.description: Rankings shifted dramatically across training horizons, proving short-run screening is unreliable for final selection
ranking_evolution_across_horizons.H23_disable_second_moment: Rank #1 at 200 steps -> Rank #4 (LOSE) at 1000 steps. Short-horizon illusion.
ranking_evolution_across_horizons.H18_simplified_second_moment: Rank #1 on toy -> Rank #1 at depth=4 -> Rank #14 (WORST) at GPT-2. Complete reversal.
ranking_evolution_across_horizons.H02_trust_ratio: Rank #2 at 200 steps -> Rank #1 at 1000 steps. Consistent and durable.
ranking_evolution_across_horizons.merged_P5_P3: Added complexity (clip+gate) did not improve over trust ratio alone at long horizon.

negative_knowledge.finding: Post-orthogonal momentum placement is catastrophic (-0.031 BPB at GPT-2)
negative_knowledge.cause: Orthogonalization is extremely sensitive to input quality; momentum-corrupted gradients break the Polar Express iteration

negative_knowledge.finding: Reducing ns_steps from 5 to 3 consistently hurts (-0.013 BPB at GPT-2)
negative_knowledge.cause: Orthogonalization quality directly impacts BPB; 5 Newton-Schulz iterations is the minimum for convergence
negative_knowledge.rule: NEVER reduce ns_steps without compensating mechanism

negative_knowledge.finding: Removing norm-preserving rescale from second_moment is the WORST mutation at GPT-2 (-0.049 BPB)
negative_knowledge.cause: The rescale prevents magnitude drift across layers; removing it causes hidden scale explosion at depth
negative_knowledge.meta_lesson: Toy backend gave +0.27 win, shallow real gave +0.029 win, GPT-2 gave -0.049 loss. Toy results are actively misleading.

negative_knowledge.finding: Disabling second_moment won at 200 steps (+0.011) but lost at 1000 steps (-0.005)
negative_knowledge.cause: Second moment provides long-horizon variance reduction that matters more as training progresses past initial convergence
negative_knowledge.rule: Short-horizon winners must be confirmed at 1000+ steps before promotion

negative_knowledge.finding: bfloat16 Polar Express is fastest (47ms vs 69ms) with marginal BPB win (+0.001) at 200 steps
negative_knowledge.cause: bf16 arithmetic is faster on B200 tensor cores, and the Polar Express coefficients are robust to reduced precision
negative_knowledge.rule: H10 is a speed optimization, not a BPB optimization. Worth revisiting as a compound with H02.

negative_knowledge.finding: Merging trust+clip+stateful gate added complexity but did not beat trust ratio alone at 1000 steps
negative_knowledge.cause: Clip and stateful gate introduce noise that slightly harms long-run convergence; trust ratio alone is the cleaner signal
negative_knowledge.rule: Simpler is better. Do not compound mutations unless each component independently holds at long horizon.

negative_knowledge.finding: Stateful gate alone LOST at GPT-2 200 steps (-0.001)
negative_knowledge.cause: Gate adds overhead and noise without a complementary mechanism to direct it
negative_knowledge.rule: Stateful gate only works in combination (as part of H08 compound), not standalone

meta_lessons: Toy backend is unreliable for ranking — use only for crash detection
Shallow depth (4 layers) does not predict full GPT-2 (12 layers) ranking
200-step rankings do not predict 1000-step rankings — always confirm at long horizon
The simplest winning mutation (H02: just add trust ratio) was the most durable
Code-level mutations were mostly inferior to DSL-level spec changes at this stage
Wildcard hypotheses (H23) provided the most interesting negative knowledge
8/14 mutations beat baseline at 200 steps, but only 1/3 survivors held at 1000 steps — attrition rate is high
next_steps: Promote H02 (trust ratio) as the new production baseline
Test H02 on multiple seeds (42, 137, 256) for robustness confirmation
Compound H02 + H10 (trust ratio + bf16 Polar Express) for BPB + speed win
Stage 3: Generate new hypotheses building on trust ratio as the base — explore trust schedule, per-head trust, trust-aware LR
Run 5000+ step evaluation to verify H02 holds into late training
